mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG
mission.description: CloudNativePG is a CNCF Kubernetes operator for managing PostgreSQL databases with automated deployment, high-availability failover, scaling, and backup management directly through Kubernetes APIs.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository.

```bash
helm repo add cnpg https://cloudnative-pg.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
Install the CloudNativePG operator into a dedicated namespace.

```bash
helm install cnpg cnpg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cnpg-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify operator is running
steps.description:
Check that the CloudNativePG operator pod is running.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n cnpg-system
```

> You should see the cloudnative-pg controller manager pod in Running state.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
Deploy a 3-instance PostgreSQL cluster to verify the operator works.

```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgresql.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgresql
spec:
  instances: 3
  storage:
    size: 1Gi
EOF
```
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgresql.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgresql
spec:
  instances: 3
  storage:
    size: 1Gi
EOF

steps.title: Verify PostgreSQL cluster is ready
steps.description:
Check the cluster status.

```bash
kubectl get clusters
```

> The cluster should show 3 ready instances.

uninstall.title: Uninstall CloudNativePG
uninstall.description:
Remove the operator from your cluster.

```bash
helm uninstall cnpg -n cnpg-system
```
